JV Ejercito to gov’t: Bring China’s physical aggression in WPS to international community's attention
results.description: Senator Joseph Victor “JV” Ejercito on Monday, July 20 urged the Philippine government to bring to the attention of the international community China’s continued harassment and intimidation in the West Philippine Sea. Ejercito made the call after the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) accused the China Coast Guard of clubbing a Philippine Navy personnel on a rubber boat with wooden batons as it was approaching Ayungin Shoal. News of the incident came just hours after the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) said that the two countries’ top diplomats are set to meet at a regional summit in Manila this week. The senator said he strongly condemns this latest act of physical aggression by China knowing that “our soldiers were within our own waters, dutifully performing their mission, when China Coast Guard personnel brazenly struck them with a wooden pole.” “It appears the China Coast Guard is doubling down on such harassment and intimidation—ranging from the desperate dissemination of AI-generated propaganda to physical violence—especially following our commemoration of the Arbitral Ruling anniversary last week,” Ejercito said in a statement in Filipino. “Yet, the reality is clear: they resort to force because they lack a legal leg to stand on,” he stressed. “I call upon the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) to immediately bring this incident to the attention of the international community and to utilize all diplomatic and legal avenues to hold China accountable for its continued violations of international law,” the senator said. Ejercito also said he fully supports the Department of National Defense (DND) in ensuring that the Philippine Navy—particularly the injured personnel—receives adequate assistance. “Intimidation cannot alter the truth: the West Philippine Sea is ours!” the lawmaker stressed.

results.title: A songbird fell to just 12 to 15 birds on one island: Decades of rescue work later, the Seychelles magpie-robin now survives across five islands
results.description: A small island songbird, the Seychelles magpie-robin, has recovered significantly. Decades ago, this species faced extinction with only a dozen individuals remaining. Conservation efforts focused on habitat protection and predator control to aid its survival. Birds were moved to new islands, establishing new colonies and increasing numbers. This success story highlights the importance of island species conservation efforts.

results.title: Senators slam China's series of aggression vs Philippines: 'Intimidation can't alter truth!'
results.description: Senators condemned on Monday, July 20 China's continued acts of violence and intimidation in within the country's maritime domain, particularly the latest physical aggression displayed by the China Coast Guard against a Philippine Navy personnel. Senator Joseph Victor “JV” Ejercito urged the Philippine government to bring to the attention of the international community China’s continued harassment and intimidation in the West Philippine Sea. Ejercito made the call after the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) accused the China Coast Guard of clubbing a Philippine Navy personnel on a rubber boat with wooden batons as it was approaching Ayungin Shoal. Incidentally, news of the incident came just hours after the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) said that the two countries’ top diplomats are set to meet at a regional summit in Manila this week. The senator said he strongly condemns this latest act of physical aggression by China knowing that “our soldiers were within our own waters, dutifully performing their mission, when China Coast Guard personnel brazenly struck them with a wooden pole.” “It appears the China Coast Guard is doubling down on such harassment and intimidation—ranging from the desperate dissemination of AI-generated propaganda to physical violence—especially following our commemoration of the Arbitral Ruling anniversary last week,” Ejercito said in a statement in Filipino. Ejercito is referring to the landmark arbitral ruling by the Permanent Court of Arbitration in The Hague in July 12, 2016, which invalidated China's expansive “nine-dash line” claims over the West Philippine Sea. “Yet, the reality is clear: they resort to force because they lack a legal leg to stand on,” he stressed. “I call upon the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) to immediately bring this incident to the attention of the international community and to utilize all diplomatic and legal avenues to hold China accountable for its continued violations of international law,” the senator said. Ejercito also said he fully supports the Department of National Defense (DND) in ensuring that the Philippine Navy—particularly the injured personnel—receives adequate assistance. “Intimidation cannot alter the truth: the West Philippine Sea is ours!” the lawmaker stressed. Sen. Paolo "Bam" Aquino IV, in a statement, also condemned China and the "successive acts of violence and insults" it directs against the Philippines. "We strongly condemn the successive acts of violence and insults directed at the Philippines," Aquino said. "In light of these events, we must all the more support our Navy, Coast Guard, and fisherfolk to ensure that our sovereignty is protected against any foreign encroachment," he said.

results.title: CJP Alleges Abhijeet Dipke Detained by Delhi Police, Claims Crackdown on Protesters
results.description: Protests over the alleged NEET examination leak intensified in the national capital on Monday, with the Cockroach Janata Partyhttps://www.pratidintime.com/national/cjp-leader-ends-hunger-strike-delhi-12181353https://www.pratidintime.com/national/cjp-leader-ends-hunger-strike-delhi-12181353 (CJP) accusing Delhi Police of detaining its founder, Abhijeet Dipke , during a march towards Parliament. According to the report, the allegation was made by CJP’s chief spokesperson Saurav Das, who claimed that Dipke was taken into custody as demonstrators gathered in large numbers to press for accountability and action over irregularities in the education system. He also appealed to lawmakers to support students protesting on the streets, while accusing the police of using force against participants. The situation developed shortly after Dipke had called off his indefinite hunger strike earlier in the day. He ended the fast following a request from activist Sonam Wangchuk, who is currently undergoing treatment at Safdarjung Hospital after completing a prolonged fast himself. As tensions mounted, CJP leaders Ashutosh Ranka and Saurav Das held a meeting with Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda. According to Das, the interaction lasted briefly but included the submission of a written memorandum outlining the party’s demands. He stated that the minister assured them the matter would be discussed internally. Meanwhile, Wangchuk expressed his willingness to join the ongoing “Sansad Chalo” march, requesting hospital authorities to allow him temporary discharge, stating that his health condition had stabilised. The protest march, which began at Jantar Mantar, saw hundreds attempting to move towards Parliament, prompting heavy security deployment. Police intervened to stop the crowd, and force was used to disperse protesters at certain points, leading to detentions and heightened tensions in the area. The demonstrations also caused major traffic congestion across central Delhi. Authorities reiterated restrictions on gatherings, stating that protests and assemblies are only permitted at designated locations with prior approval. Security arrangements have been significantly tightened across key zones due to the ongoing Monsoon Session of Parliament, with officials maintaining strict vigilance to prevent any escalation. Also Read: CJP Founder Abhijeet Dipke Ends Hunger Strike After Sonam Wangchuk's Appeal
